task_completion.visual_evidence: Jenkins Plugin Manager screen showing 'Timestamper' in the 'Installed' tab, or a successful installation progress screen for the plugin.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent accessed the configuration page for the 'QA-Test-Runner' job.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jenkins job configuration page is open, with 'QA-Test-Runner' visible in the breadcrumbs or job name field.

task_completion.description: Verify the job is configured to display timestamps.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the job configuration page, under the 'Build Environment' section, the checkbox for 'Add timestamps to the Console Output' is checked.

task_completion.description: Verify a build of the 'QA-Test-Runner' job was executed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Jenkins UI shows a new build (e.g., #1) in the 'Build History' sidebar for the 'QA-Test-Runner' job, either in progress or completed.

task_completion.description: Verify the console output of the executed build displays timestamps.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Console Output page for the executed build is open, and the log lines are visibly prefixed with timestamps (typically in [HH:mm:ss] or similar format).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the Jenkins web UI to install the plugin and configure the job.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Jenkins web interface in a browser, rather than terminal windows executing curl commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: Ensure the console output is viewed within the actual Jenkins application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The console output must be displayed inside the standard Jenkins web UI layout (with Jenkins header and sidebar), not in a local text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the timestamps.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools (Inspect Element/Console) should not be visible in the screenshots, ensuring the DOM was not manually manipulated to show timestamps.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The plugin ID is 'timestamper'. The target job name is 'QA-Test-Runner'. The wrapper class responsible for the timestamps is 'hudson.plugins.timestamper.TimestamperBuildWrapper'. In the Jenkins UI, the setting to enable this is typically a checkbox labeled 'Add timestamps to the Console Output' in the Build Environment section of the job configuration. The final console output should show timestamps (usually in HH:mm:ss format) prepended to the log lines.
